title: Client Configs
text:
---
title: Client Configurations
description: Configuration recipes for CLI coding tools using NRP-managed LLMs
---
import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Pre-built configurations for popular coding CLIs. The endpoint and token setup is covered on [API Access](/documentation/userdocs/ai/llm-managed/api-access) — start there if you don't have a token yet.

Use the right-hand "On this page" navigation to jump to your client. Every config below assumes:

- **Endpoint:** `https://ellm.nrp-nautilus.io/v1` (Anthropic-compatible at `/anthropic`)
- **Token:** the value from the [LLM token page](/llmtoken), passed in `Authorization: Bearer …` or whatever the client's API-key field expects.
- For `cache_salt`, this needs to be on the same level as `chat_template_kwargs`, but not inside. Example: `{"cache_salt": "YWJjZGVmZ2hpamtsbW5vcHFyc3R1dnd4eXphYmNkZWZnaGlqa2xtbm9wcQ==", "chat_template_kwargs": {"enable_thinking": true, "thinking": true, "preserve_thinking": true, "clear_thinking": false}}`

<Aside type="tip" title="Picking a model">
Run `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>" https://ellm.nrp-nautilus.io/v1/models` to list active model IDs, or browse the [Available Models](/documentation/userdocs/ai/llm-managed/models) feature matrix to pick one.
</Aside>

## VS Code

https://code.visualstudio.com

VS Code can use NRP-managed LLMs through the **Chat: Manage Language Models** command. This lets you pick NRP models from the model selector in Copilot Chat.

1. Open the Command Palette (`Ctrl+Shift+P` / `Cmd+Shift+P`) and run **Chat: Manage Language Models**.
2. Click **Add Models**.
3. Choose **Custom Endpoint**.
4. Enter your endpoint URL: `https://ellm.nrp-nautilus.io/v1/chat/completions`
